perturbation.original_text: XSPA shall pay to Calm on a monthly basis an amount equal to (i) fifty percent (50%) of the Retail Price for all Products sold in the Stores in the Territory during the applicable month minus (ii) fifty percent (50%) of any commission actually paid or payable to XSPA employee(s) or contractor(s) attributable to sales of such Products during such month; provided that in no event shall such commission be greater than fifteen percent (15%) of the Retail Price for the applicable Product.
perturbation.changed_text: XSPA shall pay to Calm on a monthly basis an amount equal to (i) fifty percent (50%) of the Retail Price for all Products sold in the Stores in the Territory during the applicable month minus (ii) fifty percent (50%) of any commission actually paid or payable to XSPA employee(s) or contractor(s) attributable to sales of such Products during such month; provided that in no event shall such commission be greater than twenty-five percent (25%) of the Retail Price for the applicable Product.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 8.01 modifies the maximum commission payable to XSPA employees from fifteen percent (15%) to twenty-five percent (25%) of the Retail Price. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because while section 8.01 now reflects a higher percentage, the rest of the agreement continues to operate under the original fifteen percent restriction. This discrepancy makes it unclear which commission limit applies, causing potential financial disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to Products sold in Stores in the Territory, XSPA shall deliver to Calm within twenty (20) days after each calendar month, a statement ("Monthly Statement") identifying (i) the total sales of each Product during said calendar month and (ii) the total commission paid to XSPA employees or contractors under Section ​8.02, which shall include a breakdown by each individual Product and Store. Each Monthly Statement shall be fully completed and signed and certified as accurate by one of XSPA's senior officers.
perturbation.justification: The modified text increases the maximum commission payable to XSPA employees from 15% to 25%. The contradicted text requires a monthly statement identifying the total commission paid to XSPA employees or contractors under Section 8.02. Since Section 8.01 (as modified) now allows for commissions up to 25%, while the rest of the agreement may still operate under the original 15% restriction as per the given explanation, a contradiction exists. The Monthly Statement in the Contradicted Text now reflects a higher percentage than it would have prior to the change. This represents a direct conflict because the contradicted text refers to commissions calculated under a potentially different (lower, pre-amendment) maximum, leading to uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: Calm or its authorized designees shall be the sole suppliers to XSPA of XSPA's requirements for Products, Gifts, Shopping Bags, Uniforms and Inserts (collectively, "Product Collateral") for each Store throughout the Territory. Calm shall use reasonable efforts to fulfill all of XSPA's requirements for Product Collateral in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Calm or its authorized designees shall be the sole suppliers to XSPA of XSPA's requirements for Products, Gifts, Shopping Bags, and Inserts (collectively, "Product Collateral") for each Store throughout the Territory. XSPA can source Uniforms independent of Calm. Calm shall use reasonable efforts to fulfill all of XSPA's requirements for Product Collateral in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes Section 5.05 by allowing XSPA to source Uniforms independently of Calm. The definition of "Product Collateral" now excludes Uniforms, creating a contradiction because the original definition included them. This change in definition creates an in-text contradiction. Now XSPA, doesn't have to get uniforms from Calm. This can cause problems in the collaborative efforts for marketing, making the employees to wear different uniforms with different branding. There can be issues with the trademarking.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to each Store in the Territory, XSPA shall use commercially reasonable efforts to: (a) promptly after the Effective Date, (i) prepare notices and consents and take all other actions reasonably necessary to obtain and maintain approval as necessary for bulkhead signage from airport regulatory authorities or other entities whose approval is required for such Store and install such bulkhead signage as soon as practicable thereafter, and (ii) once installed, maintain such bulkhead signage throughout the Term; 2 Source: XPRESSPA GROUP, INC., 10-K, 4/1/2019 (b) allocate at least the retail space for the display of the Products in Stores as set forth on Exhibit B; (c) cause any and all XSPA employees working in such Store to wear uniforms co-branded with Calm's and XSPA's Marks (as defined herein), including shirts and lanyards (such uniform, which will be jointly designed by and mutually acceptable to the Parties, the "Uniforms") during normal working hours; (d) display Calm branded marketing materials in such Store (which marketing materials shall be highly visible beyond the lease line as determined in Calm's sole discretion), including the items set forth on Exhibit C; (e) distribute free of charge as free gifts with purchase certain Calm branded gift products (the "Gifts"), as mutually selected by Calm and XSPA; (f) distribute free trial or discount inserts for Calm digital product subscriptions (the "Inserts") to customers of such Store (it being understood that such Inserts shall contain a unique promotional code enabling Calm to attribute purchases to such Store); and (g) use and distribute to any purchaser of any Product sold at the Stores shopping bag(s) co-branded with Calm's and XSPA's Marks (the "Shopping Bags"). Calm will collaborate with XSPA on the design of Shopping Bags and such design shall be mutually agreed by the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5.05 to allow XSPA to source Uniforms independently, which directly contradicts section (c) of the contradicted text. The contradicted text explicitly states: "(c) cause any and all XSPA employees working in such Store to wear uniforms co-branded with Calm's and XSPA's Marks...", implying XSPA is obligated to use co-branded uniforms which would be sourced through Calm as described in the original text. However, the changed text now allows XSPA to source uniforms independently. This is a clear contradiction because XSPA is now free to choose uniforms that may not be co-branded, which conflicts with the original requirement for co-branded uniforms. This falls under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Any arbitration commenced with respect to a dispute or claim under this Agreement shall be conducted pursuant to the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association and the Supplementary Procedures for Large, Complex Disputes then in effect (the "Rules"), except to the extent such rules conflict with this Section ​13. In any arbitration, New York law shall govern, except to the extent that such law conflicts with the Rules or this Section ​13. The Parties further agree that each issue submitted for arbitration be submitted to a panel of three (3) impartial arbitrators with each Party selecting one (1) arbitrator within fifteen (15) days after the commencement of the arbitration period and the two (2) selected arbitrators selecting a third arbitrator who is experienced in the commercial retail industry within thirty (30) days after the commencement of the arbitration period.
perturbation.changed_text: Any arbitration commenced with respect to a dispute or claim under this Agreement shall be conducted pursuant to the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association and the Supplementary Procedures for Large, Complex Disputes then in effect (the "Rules"), except to the extent such rules conflict with this Section ​13. In any arbitration, Delaware law shall govern, except to the extent that such law conflicts with the Rules or this Section ​13. The Parties further agree that each issue submitted for arbitration be submitted to a single arbitrator that is experienced in the commercial retail industry within thirty (30) days after the commencement of the arbitration period.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation alters Section 13 to specify that Delaware law governs the arbitration instead of New York law, and it changes the composition of the arbitration panel from three arbitrators to a single arbitrator. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement initially states in Section 16.02 that New York law shall govern the agreement. This could create confusion about which law actually applies, making the legal requirements for arbitration ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with the law of the State of New York. In the event of any litigation, arbitration, or other proceeding by which one Party either seeks to enforce its rights under this Agreement or seeks a declaration of any rights or obligations under this Agreement, the prevailing Party shall be entitled to recover from the other Party, in addition to any other relief awarded, any and all costs and expenses incurred with respect to such litigation, arbitration or other proceeding, including without limitation, reasonable attorneys' fees, disbursements and costs, and experts' fees and costs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies that Delaware law governs the arbitration, while the contradicted text states that New York law governs the entire agreement. This creates a direct conflict regarding which law applies to the agreement and any related arbitration, satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations, leading to disputes.
